Top Spirit · AngelServ01 / 17
The starting point · from your expectations paper

Where you are today

  • Reports are built by hand from ERP exports and Excel
  • Valuable know-how and business logic is not yet written down
  • The next ERP decision is open and needs vendor-neutral advice
  • You asked for data rules and human approval from day one

Each point above comes from your own expectations paper. This plan answers it point by point.

The starting point02 / 17
The outcome

What you get

I.
Time back
AI tools draft the routine reports on orders, stock, and customers. Your people check and approve them. A job of hours becomes a short review.
II.
One agreed set of numbers
Net sales, gross profit, and depletions mean the same thing in every meeting. One glossary, one truth, every report.
III.
A team that uses AI well
Every department gets a champion and a backup, and a one-page skill map showing who knows what. The skill stays in the company, not stuck with one person.
IV.
Decisions you can act on
Your processes documented, department by department, and the facts you need for the ERP decision when you are ready to take it.

We bring a working dashboard preview, built on sample files shaped like your exports, and show it live in the meeting.

The outcome03 / 17
The work

The work we propose

  • Map the work: how orders, invoices, and stock really flow, department by department
  • Remove double-work: AI drafts it, your person approves it, then it counts
  • Train the teams: every department learns to use AI tools confidently and safely
  • Build dashboards: the files you already export become clear, current reports
  • Prepare the ERP decision: vendor-neutral advice, when you are ready

Where automation justifies a real connection between systems, we build it. Until then, exported files keep it simple, safe, and within budget.

The work04 / 17
Where we would start

Three quick wins

  • The depletion report to your brand partners: built once, refreshed in minutes
  • Price lists per customer group: updated from one source file, checked by the owner
  • "What did we sell to gastronomy in May?" Answered in minutes
The approval rule, once and for all
AI drafts. Your person approves. Nothing reaches a customer, a partner, or the books unchecked.
Where we would start05 / 17
How we work

How we work, and why it is safe

  • We build and test on copies of exported files, not inside your live systems
  • Anything that writes into a live system is switched on only by your people, after testing
  • The AI tool is chosen with your IT in week one, from what your Microsoft plan already covers
  • Until IT confirms data handling, no customer data enters any AI tool
  • Everything we build stays yours, documented, built together with your team
How we work06 / 17
AI training rollout · 1 of 3

AI training in three phases

Phase 1 · Foundations
Ground rules first
Together we set the rules: what may go into AI tools, what never does. A short survey shows where each team stands. No test, no pressure.
Phase 2 · Training
Small groups, real tasks
Office and order desk, sales, finance, logistics, leadership. Each group practices on its own weekly work, not on toy examples.
Phase 3 · Making it stick
Part of normal work
One go-to colleague per department, a weekly open hour for questions, results shared with everyone once a month.

The three phases run inside the same first 90 days as the rest of the plan. The weekly open hour then continues as a standing routine.

AI training rollout07 / 17
AI training rollout · 2 of 3

What a training session looks like

  • 90 minutes, a group of four to six colleagues
  • Everyone brings one real task from their own week
  • A supplier email, a price list, a monthly report: we do it together with the AI tool
  • Every session ends with each person naming one task they now do faster

No classroom theory. The training happens on the work itself, so every session produces real finished work.

AI training rollout08 / 17
AI training rollout · 3 of 3

What your team can do after

  • Write and translate business text in German and English
  • Summarize long documents in minutes, not hours
  • Prepare first drafts of analyses and reports
  • Draft replies to customers and suppliers faster
  • Ask questions of your own sales data in plain words

The goal is to shrink the boring work, not the team. We build up the people who already run the business, and your champions carry it on without us. What you do with the freed hours is your decision.

AI training rollout09 / 17
Your team

We build up your people, not around them

I.
We map who knows what
A one-page skill map per department shows the tasks each role owns and where only one person can do them. The single points of failure, in plain sight.
II.
A champion and a backup
Each department picks a champion and a backup. They get the first hands-on practice and answer the day-to-day questions, so the skill is shared, not stuck with one person.
III.
From a few heroes to a team that knows
Today a few people carry how things really work. We turn that into written, repeatable steps the whole team can follow, and measure.
IV.
The skill stays with you
By the end your champions run the next round of training without us. AI shrinks the dull work; your people keep the know-how.
Your team10 / 17
Reporting rollout · 1 of 3

From hand-built reports to dashboards

  • Today: the same reports rebuilt by hand from exports, again and again
  • Tomorrow: dashboards your reporting team refreshes from the export folder
  • We start with the one report everyone dreads
  • Rebuild it once, count the hours saved, then take the next one
  • Power BI Desktop is Microsoft's free reporting tool: no license for the pilot

If you later want automatic sharing across the company, that is a separate license decision, made with a business case first. That is your own rule, and we follow it.

Reporting rollout11 / 17
Reporting rollout · 2 of 3

One agreed definition per number

  • Today "net sales" can mean different things in different departments
  • Same with gross profit and depletions
  • We write down one agreed definition per key figure
  • Every report then shows the same truth
  • Meetings spend time on decisions, not on whose number is right

The glossary stays with your team and moves with you into the next ERP, whichever one you choose.

Reporting rollout12 / 17
Reporting rollout · 3 of 3

What you see at the end

  • One screen with the numbers that matter
  • Sales by brand and channel, open orders, and stock cover on your top products before the Christmas quarter
  • Routine questions answered at a glance, not with a request and a wait
  • Built together with your reporting people, so they can extend it without us
  • Runs on your export files, so it survives the ERP change
Reporting rollout13 / 17
The plan

The first 90 days

Weeks 1 to 2
Ground rules
Data rules agreed, the AI tool chosen with your IT, every hand-built report listed and timed. From then on we count in real hours, not estimates.
Weeks 3 to 6
First wins
First training rounds run. The first report rebuilt with its owner and in regular use. A short prioritized list of automation opportunities, each with the hours it would save.
Weeks 7 to 12
Scale and decide
The next departments trained, first automations approved by your people, a results review with leadership, and a vendor-neutral ERP recommendation you can take forward when you are ready.

What it needs from you: about two hours per department head in weeks 1 to 2, 90 minutes per employee for training, and one folder of export files. The pilot's scope and cost are agreed in the meeting, with no software license cost. You can stop at any point and keep everything built so far.

The plan14 / 17
Proof, not promises

How we measure success

  • Hours saved per task, timed in week one, logged from then on
  • The new reports are actually used, not just delivered
  • Your processes documented, department by department
  • Your team able to use the tools without us
  • A clear ERP recommendation you can act on

These map directly to the success criteria in your expectations paper. The pilot's first target: cut the build time of the chosen report at least in half. A report someone rebuilds every month, a day each time, is about twelve working days a year. Halving that is real time back, and this is one report of several we take in turn.

Proof, not promises15 / 17
Fair questions

Questions you may already have

Will AI replace people here?
The approval rule keeps every decision and every customer relationship with your people. The aim is to shrink repetitive typing and checking. What you do with the freed hours is your call, not ours.
Is our data safe?
Customer and pricing data only enters tools your IT has approved. Nothing goes into public AI tools. Written rules say what may enter which tool, agreed with your IT in week one.
Why not wait for the new ERP?
Waiting means months more of the same manual work. Everything here runs on export files and agreed definitions, so it moves with you into whichever ERP you choose, and it makes that choice easier.
Who are you, and what if you leave?
We work as a team, and everything is documented as it is built. Everything stays with Top Spirit too: the process maps, the glossary, the dashboards, and a trained go-to colleague in each department. Built so your team can run it on their own.
Fair questions16 / 17
Next step

A working meeting at your office

  • 45 minutes, at your office, at your convenience
  • We walk this plan together, page by page
  • You choose the pilot department and the first report
  • We agree the scope and cost of the pilot together
  • We bring the working dashboard preview and show it live

The meeting carries no cost and no obligation. Paid work starts only when you approve the pilot. Start soon and the ground rules and first dashboard are in place before your busiest season, the Christmas quarter.
